one_line_ask: Turn the client's hero shot and pop-art logo into a reusable duotone/halftone social launch kit: stickers, icons, carousel + reel-cover art, platform-sized posts, and data-merged post variations — all built on Adobe connector tools.
brief: Our brand identity is built on colorful pop-art, and we're launching across Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn and Pinterest. We already have our logo and brand identity (logo supplied as a layered Illustrator file with Variables; brand colors anchored on a hot-magenta + cyan duotone pair). Build the pop-art social launch kit from a single hero shot we provide.\n\nStart by isolating the hero subject (remove the background to a clean transparent PNG). Convert that cutout into our signature look: a bold halftone/duotone treatment (visible print-screen dots) recolored into the brand magenta/cyan pair, then push the pop with a punchy color overlay and an HSL boost so the magentas and cyans sing. From that same pop-art subject, derive a scalable sticker/icon asset by vectorizing it to clean SVG (so we can recolor and reuse it forever without quality loss). Produce a reel-cover canvas by extending the artwork to a tall 9:16 frame (outpaint the edges so nothing important is cropped), and then cut the finished pop-art hero into the four platform sizes we post at: 1:1 feed, 4:5 portrait, 9:16 story/reel, and 2:3 Pinterest. Export our supplied logo file to web-ready PNG/SVG/PDF so it drops into eve
